skill.name: creative-media
skill.description: Creates and manages generated media through the hosted Image Skill CLI and API, including images, edits, video, audio, and image-to-3D asset workflows.
skill.summary: Generate creative media through a hosted Image Skill runtime.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: Static external-command, network, and filesystem findings are real operational behaviors documented by the skill. They appear tied to legitimate hosted media workflows, but they require user awareness because commands pull npm packages, contact hosted endpoints, and can write agent state under hidden directories. The weak-cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 19 are false positives; those lines are YAML description text and contain no cryptographic implementation.

medium_findings.title: Remote CLI Execution Required for Core Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to run npm and npx commands, including latest-version package execution and install commands. This is expected for the hosted media runtime, but it creates supply-chain and command-execution risk if the package or install source is compromised.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and runnable, and line 28 also tells agents when to execute a generated next command. The intent appears legitimate, but the risk is real because execution depends on external packages.

medium_findings.title: Hosted API and Durable URL Dependency
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill hardcodes Image Skill web, documentation, contract, and API URLs. These endpoints are central to the skill, but generated prompts, job state, feedback, payment state, or media metadata may be sent to an external hosted service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plainly documented as the canonical hosted service and API. I did not find evidence of malicious exfiltration, but network transfer is part of the intended workflow.

medium_findings.title: Agent State Writes to Hidden Directories
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill documents writes to $HOME/.agents and sets CODEX_HOME to $HOME/.codex for installation fallback. This is normal agent configuration behavior, but it can modify hidden profile state and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden directory paths are explicit and associated with installation commands. The instructions are transparent, so this is a moderate operational concern rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators at SKILL.md lines 3 and 19. Line 3 begins a YAML description block, and line 19 describes an optional restricted token; no hashing, encryption, or weak cryptographic algorithm is implemented there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain documentation text only. I found no code or cryptographic operation at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Executes Remote Packages
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx to execute image-skill and skills installer packages. Users should review package provenance and avoid running spend-producing commands without approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are directly present in the skill. The danger is supply-chain exposure, not evidence of malicious command content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Next Command May Be Executed
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells agents to run data.next_command only when it is marked copy-runnable and media spend is allowed. This guard reduces risk, but executing generated commands still needs user oversight.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly references running a generated command with conditions. The conditions are useful safeguards, but the pattern remains sensitive.

content.user_title: Generate Creative Media Through Image Skill
content.value_statement: Creative teams need one workflow for generated images, edits, video, audio, and 3D assets. This skill routes agents through Image Skill with hosted URLs, job recovery, receipts, and structured command output.
actual_capabilities: Provides first-run and model-list commands for the Image Skill CLI.
Supports image generation, image editing, video, audio, and image-to-3D workflows through the hosted runtime.
Documents canonical service, API, CLI, and LLM contract URLs.
Guides agents to use no-spend checks before running media creation commands.
Explains install commands for the creative-media and canonical image-skill entries.
Documents fallback setup for writable HOME and CODEX_HOME paths.
limitations: Requires network access to the hosted Image Skill service and npm package sources.
May require credits or spend approval before media creation commands run.
Does not include local model weights or an offline generation runtime.
Depends on the capabilities and availability of the hosted Image Skill platform.

use_cases.title: Generate Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Create draft images and media assets with hosted URLs and receipts for review.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Media
use_cases.description: Explore image edits, short video concepts, or 3D asset directions before production work.

use_cases.title: Automate Media Requests
use_cases.description: Use structured CLI responses so agents can recover jobs, cite outputs, and track costs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a First Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a no-spend guide first.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use creative-media to prepare an image generation request for a clean product photo. Run the guide first and wait before spending credits.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Models
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose a suitable hosted model before generation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use creative-media to list available models as JSON. Recommend options for image editing and explain any spend or latency considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a supplied visual while keeping output recoverable.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use creative-media to plan an image edit for this asset. Preserve job details, hosted output URLs, and cost receipt information.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Modal Asset Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow across image, video, audio, and 3D.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use creative-media to design a staged asset workflow for image, short video, audio, and image-to-3D outputs. Use no-spend checks before each creation step.

output_examples.input: Generate a studio product image for a compact field camera.
output_examples.output: The agent starts with a guide command, confirms required inputs, waits for spend approval, then returns hosted output links and a cost receipt.

output_examples.input: Find models that can edit an uploaded image.
output_examples.output: The agent lists available models, highlights edit-capable choices, and notes any missing capability or fallback path.

output_examples.input: Create a campaign asset set with image and short video variations.
output_examples.output: The agent proposes staged commands, separates no-spend checks from creation steps, and keeps job recovery details for later agents.

best_practices: Run guide or model-list commands before any media creation step.
Confirm spend approval before executing generated next commands.
Keep hosted URLs, job identifiers, and receipts with the project notes.
anti_patterns: Do not paste provider API keys into prompts or CLI commands.
Do not run generated creation commands when spend approval is unclear.
Do not rely on this skill for offline or private local generation.

faq.question: Does this skill generate media locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the hosted Image Skill runtime and CLI rather than local model servers.

faq.question: Does it require provider API keys?
faq.answer: The skill says provider API keys are not required. It uses Image Skill identity and hosted service access.

faq.question: Can it create images, video, audio, and 3D assets?
faq.answer: The skill documents workflows for image generation, image editing, video, audio, and image-to-3D assets.

faq.question: Is spending automatic?
faq.answer: The skill instructs agents to run no-spend checks first and only run creation commands when media spend is allowed.

faq.question: Why does the audit show medium risk?
faq.answer: The workflow uses remote CLI execution, hosted network services, and hidden agent state paths. These are expected but sensitive behaviors.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should review the npm package source, hosted service terms, requested spend, and any generated next command.
